input.background: An electric network has $N$ relays. If a relay is tripped, the repair time is a random variable that obeys the exponential distribution with a constant failure rate parameter $\lambda=19 / \mathrm{h}$. If one repairman is available and ten relays have tripped simultaneously.
input.question: What is the probability that power failure will last more than $\frac{3}{4} \mathrm{~h}$?

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
